description: Multi-N tensor-closure-gap series across nine lattice anchors (N in [409.5, 28014.5]). The tensor-closure gap is the macroclass-closure deficit of the D1 module and is available on all nine anchors via the bundled stencils; the more rigorous Einstein-identity gap is reported separately and is currently only available for the canonical and extended regimes.

definition.einstein_identity_gap: the Einstein-identity gap; available only for the canonical and extended regimes on the bundled stencils

two_point_einstein_identity_gap_canonica.note: These two-point values use the rigorous Einstein-identity gap. Extending to the seven additional lattice anchors requires producing the corresponding lattice runs for those regimes, which is deferred.

verdict: Tensor-closure-gap series: 9 points across N in [409.5, 28014.5]. Free-fit converges to gap_inf around 0.27-0.32, scaling exponent alpha around 0.5-1.0 (full grid search; numerically the gap is weakly N-dependent at the available resolution). The two-point Einstein-identity-gap series (canonical, extended) remains the rigorous result with Richardson gap_inf < 0.05 at all three canonical exponents (2/3, 1, 0.848).
